skill.name: debugging-toolkit-smart-debug
skill.description: AI-powered debugging specialist for root cause analysis, observability-driven investigation, and production-safe issue resolution
skill.summary: Systematic debugging workflow with AI-assisted hypothesis generation, observability data collection, and validated fix implementation
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings were determined to be false positives. The flagged patterns are markdown syntax (backticks for inline code), YAML frontmatter, and documentation examples - not executable code. Line 23 uses backticks for file path reference in markdown. Lines 147-184 contain TypeScript documentation examples illustrating the debugging workflow. No actual command execution, cryptographic operations, or network reconnaissance code exists in this skill.

content.user_title: Debug production issues with AI-assisted root cause analysis
content.value_statement: Reduce mean time to resolution for production incidents by combining systematic debugging workflows with AI-powered pattern recognition. Get structured hypothesis generation, observability data correlation, and validated fix recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Analyze error messages, stack traces, and failure patterns to identify affected components
Guide observability data collection from Sentry, DataDog, Jaeger, ELK, and similar platforms
Generate ranked hypotheses with probability scores and supporting evidence
Recommend appropriate debugging strategies based on issue characteristics
Design production-safe instrumentation with conditional breakpoints and feature-flagged logging
Validate fixes through test coverage, performance comparison, and canary deployment monitoring
limitations: Requires access to observability platform credentials and log aggregation systems
Cannot directly execute debugging tools or modify production systems
Effectiveness depends on quality and completeness of available observability data
Does not replace human judgment for critical production incidents requiring immediate escalation

use_cases.title: Production incident triage
use_cases.description: Rapidly analyze incoming production alerts, correlate errors across services, and generate initial hypotheses for on-call engineers to investigate
use_cases.target_user: On-call engineers and SREs responding to production incidents

use_cases.title: Intermittent bug investigation
use_cases.description: Apply statistical debugging techniques to identify patterns in failures affecting small percentage of users or requests
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers troubleshooting race conditions and timing-dependent issues

use_cases.title: Post-mortem root cause analysis
use_cases.description: Reconstruct execution paths, identify contributing factors, and generate prevention recommendations for incident documentation
use_cases.target_user: Engineering managers and tech leads conducting incident reviews

prompt_templates.title: Basic error analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this error and identify the most likely root cause: [paste error message or stack trace]. Include the affected component and recommended first debugging step.

prompt_templates.title: Hypothesis generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 3-5 ranked hypotheses for this issue: [describe symptoms]. For each hypothesis, provide probability score, supporting evidence needed, and how to falsify it.

prompt_templates.title: Observability query design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design queries to investigate [specific issue] across [list platforms: Sentry, DataDog, logs]. Include specific filters, time ranges, and correlation keys to identify affected users and patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Production-safe debugging plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a debugging plan for [issue] that can be executed in production without impacting users. Include instrumentation points, feature flag design, sampling strategy, and rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: Checkout timeout errors affecting 5% of production traffic, intermittent
output: Issue Summary: Payment processing timeouts occurring in 5% of checkout requests
Top Hypothesis (75%): N+1 query pattern in payment method verification - supported by trace analysis showing 15+ sequential database calls
Debugging Strategy: Add span attributes for query count and payment method ID, deploy canary to 10% traffic
Validation: Compare latency percentiles and query counts between control and canary groups

output_examples.input: Memory leak suspected in background job processor
output: Issue Summary: Gradual memory growth in job processor pods, restart required every 6 hours
Top Hypothesis (60%): Unbounded cache growth without eviction policy
Debugging Strategy: Enable heap profiling with Pyroscope, capture heap snapshots at intervals
Validation: Monitor RSS memory trends after implementing cache size limits

best_practices: Always correlate errors with deployment timelines to identify recent changes as potential causes
Use conditional instrumentation and feature flags to enable debug logging for specific user cohorts without impacting overall system performance
Document the full hypothesis testing process including falsified hypotheses to prevent future investigators from repeating the same paths
anti_patterns: Adding excessive logging without sampling or rate limiting, causing log volume spikes that obscure the signal
Attempting to reproduce complex production issues in development without first gathering sufficient observability data
Implementing fixes without establishing clear success criteria and rollback plans before deployment

faq.question: What observability platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: The workflow is platform-agnostic and applies to any observability tool. Common integrations include Sentry, DataDog, New Relic, Dynatrace, Jaeger, Zipkin, Honeycomb, ELK stack, Splunk, Loki, Pyroscope, LogRocket, and FullStory.

faq.question: Can this skill directly access my production systems?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and analysis only. You control all access to observability platforms and execute all debugging commands. The skill helps you formulate queries, interpret results, and plan next steps.

faq.question: How do I handle sensitive data in error logs during debugging?
faq.answer: Use feature-flagged logging to enable debug output only for specific test users or internal accounts. Ensure logs are scrubbed of PII before analysis. Consider using production-safe techniques like read-only debug endpoints with authentication.

faq.question: What if the AI-generated hypotheses are all incorrect?
faq.answer: The workflow includes falsification criteria for each hypothesis. If all initial hypotheses are ruled out, use the evidence gathered to generate new hypotheses. The structured approach ensures you build knowledge even from negative results.

faq.question: How do I know when to escalate instead of continuing investigation?
faq.answer: Escalate when: impact exceeds your authority threshold, root cause spans multiple teams, fix requires architectural changes, or investigation exceeds time budget. Document all findings before handoff.

faq.question: Can this skill help with prevention after the fix is deployed?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes prevention steps: AI-assisted regression test generation, knowledge base updates with root cause documentation, monitoring and alert setup for similar issues, and runbook updates for future incidents.
